title: The Kozuki Retainers Reveal the Truth
description: On pages 2–5, this part focuses on Unknown person #1, Vivi, Unknown person #2, Kiku, Unknown person #3, Unknown person #4 as The truth of the Kozuki retainers and Wano’s time-displaced rebellion is revealed as Kaido’s power closes in.
scene: Kinemon explains the Kozuki tragedy, the twenty-year leap, and the mission to overthrow Kaido and Orochi.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Wano Country- Kuri- The ruins of Open Castle

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: This place was called Open Castle.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Kim'Emon recalls the 'past...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: That is what the people of Kuri called the castle that once stood here...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But actually, its real name was... Kuri Castle.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Oden was beloved by the residents of Kuri. And therefore,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: The castle was called as such.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: So that was Momo's father!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: That's right!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Our master, Lord Oden, was quite the wildcard...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Despite being the son of Wano Country's Shogun, Lord Kouzuki Sukiyaki...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He got into violent fights one after the other... and was eventually exiled from the flower capital!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...However, at the time, there was a place in the Wano country that was considered a lawless area...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: 8 What...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Criminals and Ronin Exiled from their village wandered about there...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: and formed groups to rob and kill. Each day, blood was spilled.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It was such a heinous place, that even the Shogun decided to ignore its problems.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: That was Kuri.

dialogue.speaker: Kiku
dialogue.text: The young Lord Oden...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: went to that kuri, of all places.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: and started a huge fight with a man known as Ashura Douji...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: who was considered the most dangerous man out of all the evildoers in this land. In the end, Lord open defeated him.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Shortly afterwards, he rounded up the ruffians in this land, and raised them from their pitiful bad deeds...!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He forced them to learn how to be workers, as it was a necessity for the construction of this town and the castle.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He built a paradise farm for the sake of everyone... and created a free and lively village where the sounds of laughter would not stop.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It was then that he was bestowed the title of DAIMYO of Kuri... at the young age of 20... it was a monumental achievement for Lord Oden.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: After some time, Shogun Sukiyaki heard of his deeds.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: He created rivers and wharfs...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: He connected villages and people together.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Lord Oden had a habit of saying this one thing:

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: "It's too cramped here!"

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: ...Jeez, those guys...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Where in the world did they wander off to...?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'm amazed that they could get lost even while I was riding on that komanu...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Oh, it's a wharf!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Maybe I should catch a ride!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Wano Country - Kuri - ON A CERTAIN BEACH...

dialogue.speaker: Kiku
dialogue.text: for Lord Oden...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: We drifted to this land...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The Wano Country was a little bit too cramped...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: How nostalgic... now I remember. This is that beach....!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And we were referred to as the talking cat and the talking dog. Everybody here feared us.

dialogue.speaker: Kiku
dialogue.text: All of them, except for Lord Oden....!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: You all should be ashamed!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Being afraid of those who look different only highlights your ignorance!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: He was indeed our Savior....!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And was indeed a true hero...!! and more than anything, he was indeed our Lord and Master.....!!!

summary: This section advances the Wano Country - Kozuki Secret storyline with 47 dialogue beats. The Kozuki retainers reveal that Wano’s rebellion has been carried twenty years into the future.
